title: Vocabulário Académico EN para Estudantes Moçambicanos
description: 200 palavras de vocabulário académico em inglês com tradução para português de Moçambique. Útil para preparação de TOEFL, IELTS, e candidaturas a ensino superior internacional. Baseado na Academic Word List (Coxhead 2000).
source_reference: Academic Word List (AWL), Coxhead 2000, Victoria University of Wellington
audience: Estudantes moçambicanos a preparar exames de inglês académico (TOEFL, IELTS) ou candidaturas a universidades estrangeiras de língua inglesa.

schema.moz_example: Frase de exemplo contextualizada para Moçambique (opcional)

entries.definition_en: to examine something in detail to understand it
entries.example_en: Researchers analyse data to identify trends.
entries.moz_example: Researchers in Maputo analyse mobile money usage patterns.

entries.example_en: The analysis revealed three key patterns.

entries.definition_en: facts and statistics collected for reference or analysis
entries.example_en: The data shows a clear correlation.
entries.note_pt: Em inglês académico, 'data' é tratado como plural ou massa não-contável (the data show OU shows).

entries.example_en: Her research focuses on climate change.

entries.definition_en: a particular procedure for doing something
entries.example_en: The qualitative method works best here.

entries.definition_en: a system of methods used in a study
entries.example_en: The chapter explains our methodology in detail.
entries.note_pt: Não confundir com 'method'. 'Methodology' é o sistema de métodos; 'method' é uma técnica específica.

entries.definition_en: a proposed explanation to be tested
entries.example_en: The hypothesis was rejected after testing.

entries.definition_en: a factor that can change in an experiment
entries.example_en: Income is a key variable in this study.

entries.definition_en: facts or signs that support a claim
entries.example_en: There is strong evidence for this claim.

entries.definition_en: to judge the value or quality of something
entries.example_en: We evaluate the programme's impact annually.

entries.definition_en: the process of judging quality or value
to make a judgement about something

entries.example_en: We need to identify the main causes.

entries.definition_en: to explain the meaning of information
entries.example_en: How do we interpret these findings?

entries.definition_en: to reach a decision after consideration
entries.example_en: We conclude that more research is needed.
The conclusion is supported by data.

entries.definition_en: a study of opinions or experiences from many people
entries.example_en: We conducted a survey of 500 households.
entries.moz_example: A survey of 500 households in Beira showed widespread mobile money use.

entries.definition_en: a small part used to represent a whole
a mutual relationship between two things
entries.example_en: There is a strong correlation between A and B.
entries.note_pt: ATENÇÃO: 'correlation does not imply causation' (correlação não implica causalidade).

entries.example_en: Rainfall is highly variable in this region.
entries.note_pt: Como adjectivo significa 'que varia'. Como substantivo, é um factor num estudo.

entries.definition_en: to check or prove the validity of
entries.example_en: We need to validate these results.
A comprehensive review of the literature.
We propose a new framework for analysis.
This represents a paradigm shift in the field.

entries.definition_en: based on observation or experience, not theory
entries.example_en: The study uses empirical data from 2020-2025.
We can infer from the data that demand grew.
This inference is supported by multiple studies.
The study followed a rigorous methodology.
Climate change is a global phenomenon.

entries.definition_en: unlikely to have occurred by chance
an unfair preference that may distort results
entries.example_en: The study controlled for selection bias.

entries.definition_en: studying the same subjects over time
entries.example_en: A longitudinal study tracks subjects for years.

entries.definition_en: to propose an explanation to be tested
entries.example_en: We hypothesise that income affects savings.
The conceptual framework guides the research.

entries.definition_en: to give reasons for or against an idea
entries.example_en: The author argues that policy must change.

entries.definition_en: a reason given to support an idea
a statement that something is true
entries.example_en: He claims that the data is flawed.
The author asserts that reform is urgent.
Critics contend that the policy failed.
These results imply a causal relationship.
The implications for policy are significant.
There is an implicit assumption here.

entries.definition_en: to accept that something is true

entries.example_en: On the contrary, the data shows growth.
The method is old; nevertheless, it works.

entries.definition_en: as a result; in this way

entries.example_en: Hence the need for further investigation.
The new study refutes the original theory.
More data is needed to substantiate this claim.
We tested the validity of the model.

entries.definition_en: to combine ideas into a coherent whole
entries.example_en: The chapter synthesises 30 years of research.
We emphasise the need for action.
The report highlights three key issues.
Could you elaborate on that point?

entries.definition_en: giving information clearly in few words

entries.example_en: The argument is coherent and well-structured.

entries.definition_en: a set of rules or course of action by government
entries.example_en: The new education policy starts in 2027.
Mozambique held its first multiparty elections in 1994.
It is legal to register a business online.
The Banco de Moçambique regulates the currency.

entries.definition_en: a rule made by an authority
the fundamental laws of a country
a change to a document or law
process of choosing leaders by vote
entries.example_en: The next general election is in 2029.

entries.definition_en: a member of a country with rights
entries.example_en: All citizens have the right to vote at 18.

entries.definition_en: the wellbeing of a person or group
entries.example_en: The state provides social welfare programmes.

entries.moz_example: Maputo has experienced rapid urbanisation since the 1990s.

entries.definition_en: basic systems and services of a country
able to be maintained over time
the act of combining into a whole
entries.example_en: Regional integration in SADC is ongoing.
Mozambique has a diverse linguistic landscape.
Linguistic diversity is rich in Mozambique.

entries.definition_en: relating to a national or cultural group

entries.example_en: Indigenous languages are protected by law.
Policies aim to support marginalised communities.

entries.definition_en: the wealth and resources of a country
entries.example_en: The economy grew by 5% last year.

entries.definition_en: to put money into something for profit

entries.example_en: Investment in renewable energy is growing.

entries.definition_en: income, especially of a company or government
entries.example_en: Government revenue from gas exports rose.

entries.moz_example: Inflation in Mozambique reached 4% in 2025 according to the BCM.

entries.definition_en: the money used in a country
entries.example_en: The metical is the currency of Mozambique.

entries.definition_en: the value of one currency in another
entries.example_en: The MZN/USD exchange rate has been stable.
The Prime Rate is set by the central bank.

entries.definition_en: an estimate of income and expenditure
entries.example_en: The annual budget was approved in October.

entries.definition_en: the buying and selling of goods
the exchange of goods and services
entries.example_en: Trade between SADC countries is increasing.

entries.definition_en: where goods are bought and sold
entries.example_en: The market for mobile money is competitive.
Supply has not kept pace with demand.

entries.definition_en: an urgent need or desire for something
entries.example_en: Demand for clean energy is rising.

entries.definition_en: a person who starts a business
entries.example_en: Young entrepreneurs are reshaping the economy.
Real estate is a valuable asset.
The company paid a dividend in March.

entries.definition_en: money paid by the government to support an industry
a tax on imports or exports
entries.example_en: Tariffs on imported sugar were reduced.
Fiscal policy aims to control deficits.
M-Pesa was a major innovation in Africa.

entries.definition_en: a set of rules for solving a problem

entries.example_en: Data science combines statistics and computing.

entries.definition_en: to make a process operate automatically
use of technology to perform tasks
the property of being able to handle growth
entries.example_en: Scalability is critical for cloud platforms.

entries.definition_en: use of living systems for technology

entries.example_en: The human genome was first sequenced in 2003.
Subatomic particles are studied at CERN.
Cells contain DNA in their nuclei.

entries.definition_en: the smallest unit of a chemical compound
the smallest unit of an element
a student studying for a first degree
entries.example_en: She is an undergraduate at UEM.
entries.moz_example: She is an undergraduate at Universidade Eduardo Mondlane.

entries.example_en: Postgraduate studies often require a thesis.
He won a scholarship to study abroad.

entries.definition_en: a long essay for a degree
entries.example_en: Her dissertation is on rural finance.

entries.definition_en: a long piece of academic writing
entries.example_en: His thesis defends a controversial position.

entries.definition_en: the subjects taught in a course
entries.example_en: The curriculum was updated last year.

entries.definition_en: an outline of a subject's contents
entries.example_en: The syllabus lists all required readings.

entries.definition_en: the teaching staff of a university
entries.note_pt: Em PT-MZ, 'faculdade' refere-se à divisão (Faculdade de Letras), enquanto em EN 'faculty' é o staff.

entries.example_en: Every claim needs a proper citation.

entries.definition_en: a specialist in an academic subject

entries.example_en: His seminal paper changed the field.

entries.moz_example: Malaria remains a major health challenge in Mozambique.

entries.example_en: The cholera epidemic was contained quickly.
COVID-19 was declared a pandemic in 2020.

entries.definition_en: a substance that protects against disease
the body's ability to resist disease
the act of stopping something happening
the process of getting food for health

entries.example_en: Child mortality has decreased over the decades.
A clinical trial tests new drugs.

entries.definition_en: weather conditions over a long period
release of gas into the air

entries.example_en: Solar power is a renewable energy source.

entries.moz_example: Conservation efforts in Gorongosa National Park have restored wildlife.

entries.definition_en: variety of plant and animal life
a community of organisms and their environment
the natural home of an organism
to change to suit new conditions
entries.example_en: Communities must adapt to changing weather.
Mitigate the impact of floods through planning.
